senses.definition: to provide a home for a living creature and care for it

examples.x: In some cultures, it is customary to keep an animal, such as a goat, for religious ceremonies.
It's a significant responsibility to keep an animal, as it requires proper care and attention.

senses.definition: to maintain an even distribution between various elements or priorities

examples.x: It's important to keep a balance between work and personal life for overall well-being.
Managing your time effectively means keeping a balance between tasks.
Entrepreneurs strive to keep the balance between risk and reward in their ventures.
Diplomats work to keep a balance between international relationships and national interests.
Investors aim to keep the balance between stocks and bonds in their portfolios.

senses.definition: to maintain a written or digital account of information, events, or data for future reference

examples.x: The accountant needs to keep a record of all financial transactions.
Students are encouraged to keep a record of their study hours and progress.
The library keeps a record of all the books borrowed by its patrons.
To track your fitness journey, it's essential to keep a record of your workouts.

senses.definition: to record and track the points or results in a game, competition, or other similar activity

examples.x: In a tennis match, it's essential to keep score to determine the winner.
They took turns keeping score during the board game.
The referee's job is to keep score during the basketball game.
Golfers keep score of their strokes on each hole.
During the card game, she was responsible for keeping score.

word_list.pronunciation: kˈiːp tˈæbz ˌɑːn ˌɛsbˈiː slˈæʃ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ

senses.definition: to closely observe someone or something to stay informed about their activities or whereabouts

examples.x: Parents often keep tabs on their children's online activities to ensure safety.
The detective kept tabs on the suspect to gather evidence.
She kept tabs on her favorite celebrity's social media accounts.
I like to keep tabs on the stock market to make informed investments.

word_list.pronunciation: kˈiːp tɹˈæk ʌv ˌɛsbˈiː slˈæʃ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ

senses.definition: to ensure that one has the latest news concerning someone or something

examples.x: I use a calendar to keep track of my appointments and deadlines.
The app allows you to keep track of your daily steps and physical activity.
The GPS system helps us keep track of our location during the road trip.
It's important to keep track of your expenses to manage your budget effectively.
Parents can use a chore chart to keep track of their children's responsibilities.

senses.definition: used to praise someone for their efforts and encouraging them to continue performing well

examples.x: In his performance review, he was commended for his dedication and told to keep up the good work.
You've been doing an excellent job on this project, so keep up the good work.
The coach reminded his players to stay focused and keep up the good work as they headed into the championship game.

senses.definition: to maintain a physical or metaphorical separation from someone or something, often for safety, privacy, or respect

examples.x: He prefers to keep a distance from his coworkers when he's working on a project.
To avoid interfering, it's best to keep a distance when someone is going through a difficult time.
Sometimes it's necessary to keep a distance from negative influences in your life.

senses.definition: to regularly write and maintain a personal journal or record of one's thoughts, experiences, and daily activities

examples.x: Many writers keep a diary to capture their creative ideas and life experiences.
She decided to keep a diary during her travels to document her adventures.
Keeping a diary can be a therapeutic way to express emotions and reflect on life.
As a teenager, I used to keep a diary to record my feelings and experiences.
To preserve family memories, some individuals keep a diary of their family's history.

senses.definition: to regularly write and maintain a personal record of thoughts, experiences, and events

examples.x: He liked to keep a journal to document his daily life and thoughts.
Keeping a journal helped her manage stress and improve her mental well-being.
Students were encouraged to keep a journal as part of their reflective writing practice.

senses.definition: to monitor a particular area, situation, or person in order to be aware of any developments, changes, or potential dangers

examples.x: The lifeguard kept a lookout for swimmers in distress.
During the camping trip, someone needed to keep a lookout for wild animals.
He asked his friend to keep a lookout while he quickly ran into the store.
The security guard kept a lookout for suspicious activity on the surveillance cameras.
In a game of hide and seek, one child is designated to keep a lookout.

senses.definition: to maintain physical fitness and good health through regular exercise and a healthy lifestyle

examples.x: They are keeping in shape through a combination of running and swimming.
To stay competitive, athletes must work hard to keep in shape.
It's essential to eat a balanced diet to keep in shape.
She keeps in shape by going to the gym regularly.

senses.definition: to maintain a similar speed or level of progress as something or someone else

examples.x: She had to walk quickly to keep pace with her friends.
The company is striving to innovate and keep pace with industry changes.
She keeps pace with the rest of the team during training.
They are keeping pace with the market's changing trends.
While studying for the exam, she was keeping pace with the class assignments.

senses.definition: to maintain financial stability or solvency

examples.x: Despite the economic downturn, the company managed to keep afloat by cutting costs and diversifying its revenue streams.
The government provided emergency funding to help struggling businesses keep afloat during the pandemic.
The nonprofit organization relies on donations to keep afloat and continue its important work in the community.
The small bookstore struggled to keep afloat in the era of online retail giants.
During tough times, it's important to find ways to keep afloat and weather the storm.
